name: creel_search
description: Search for relevant content chunks across one or more Creel topics using semantic similarity. Use this when you need to find information related to a query. Returns ranked chunks with scores, document citations, and optionally linked content.

topic_ids.description: List of topic IDs to search within. You must specify at least one topic.

query_text.description: The natural language query to search for. This will be embedded and compared against stored chunks.

top_k.description: Maximum number of results to return. Defaults to 10.

follow_links.description: If true, also return chunks linked to the top results. Useful for discovering related content.

link_depth.description: How many hops to follow when follow_links is true. Higher values find more distant connections.

name: creel_get_context
description: Retrieve temporal context from a specific document. Returns chunks in chronological order, optionally filtered by recency. Use this when you already know which document to read and want its recent content or a time-bounded slice.

document_id.description: The ID of the document to retrieve context from.

last_n.description: Return only the last N chunks from the document. Useful for getting the most recent entries.

since.description: ISO 8601 timestamp. Only return chunks created after this time. Example: 2026-01-15T00:00:00Z

name: creel_add_memory
description: Queue a memory observation for processing. Returns a job_id; the memory maintenance worker handles deduplication. Memories persist across sessions and are scoped to the calling principal and a named scope. Optionally structure the memory as a subject/predicate/object triple for precise retrieval.

scope.description: The memory scope (namespace). Use a consistent scope name to group related memories, such as 'user_preferences' or 'project_notes'.

content.description: The memory content as natural language text. This is the primary content that will be stored and searchable.

subject.description: Optional subject of a structured triple. Example: 'Nick'

predicate.description: Optional predicate of a structured triple. Example: 'prefers'

object.description: Optional object of a structured triple. Example: 'dark mode'

name: creel_search_memories
description: Search stored memories by semantic similarity. Use this to recall previously stored observations, facts, or preferences. Optionally filter to a specific scope.

query_text.description: The natural language query to search memories for.

scope.description: Optional scope to limit the search to. If omitted, searches across all scopes.

top_k.description: Maximum number of results to return. Defaults to 10.

name: creel_list_memories
description: List all memories in a given scope. Use this to see everything stored in a scope without searching. Optionally include memories that have been invalidated.

scope.description: The memory scope to list memories from.

include_invalidated.description: If true, also return memories that have been marked as invalidated. Defaults to false.

name: creel_delete_memory
description: Permanently delete a stored memory by its ID. Use this to remove outdated or incorrect memories.

id.description: The ID of the memory to delete.

name: creel_upload_document
description: Upload text content as a new document in a topic. The content will be chunked, embedded, and indexed for future search. Use this to store new information that should be retrievable later.

topic_id.description: The ID of the topic to upload the document into.

name.description: A human-readable name for the document.

content.description: The text content to upload. This will be chunked and embedded for semantic search.

slug.description: Optional URL-friendly identifier. If omitted, one will be generated from the name.

content_type.description: MIME type of the content. Defaults to text/plain. Use text/markdown for Markdown content.

name: creel_list_topics
description: List all topics you have access to. Topics are collections of documents organized by subject area. Use this to discover what knowledge bases are available before searching.

name: creel_get_document
description: Retrieve metadata for a specific document by its ID. Returns the document name, type, topic, status, and other metadata. Does not return the document content; use creel_get_context for that.

id.description: The ID of the document to retrieve.

name: creel_list_documents
description: List all documents within a topic. Use this to see what documents are available in a topic before searching or retrieving context.

topic_id.description: The ID of the topic to list documents from.
